lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: What do you think you're doing, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Admiring these lovely gates?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You can admire them from back here!

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: It's a bit rude of 'em to take up the whole bridge with their conversation, isn't it, mate?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Aye, mate, but wouldn't it be just as rude for us to interrupt?

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: A good point well made, mate. Who are we to decide that the dig is more impor'ant than a personal disagreement between two people?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: You're not wrong, mate. We often place matters of industry above those of people, and it's chippin' away at our ability to relate to each other on a human level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: We're part of a dig into some ruins beneath Cam Torum, but the way's blocked by these folks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you tried asking them to move?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: We're considering the moral implications of doing exactly that, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you two philosophers?

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: No mate, builders.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: But thinking, talking, discussing, it's what makes us human, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not just humans that do those things.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Fair play, mate. I guess we're reflecting more on the condition of sen'ience in general then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Have a good one, mate.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Have you killed that creature yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Working on it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: It's up by the mountain outflow spring, north east of here. Get to it before it makes its way to the aqueduct!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It is my right and duty as a follower of Ranul to document that which has been found beneath your city.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Your beliefs do not grant you a right to anything in Cam Torum, not to document things, and especially not to get yourself killed.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I don't want to give Fortis any excuses to come knocking on our door to investigate your untimely and idiotic demise.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I won't take no for an answer. I am bound by my divine oath.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Oh yes? And will your divine oath feed you in there? Light your way? Keep you safe from cave-ins and monsters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know about cave-ins, but I can handle monsters.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm busy with a different arrogant human. You'll have to wait in line.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Ranul provides...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: No! We've more than enough humans in Cam Torum with this blasted Fortis mandated dig! I don't care how many deals it secured, it's not worth all this trouble!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Advisor Attala...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Apologies, but I was sent to tell you that one of the creatures from the ruins managed to bypass our defenses.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It escaped through the mountain's outflow spring, to the north east.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The one that leads to the aqueduct?! We'll have Fortis guards barging into the city if they catch wind...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like you need help.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Cam Torum does not need help, much less that of any humans!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Wake the off-duty guards and put them to work. I'll ensure they're paid handsomely.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm afraid we already have.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Argh! Get back to your post!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Alright, fine! Human, you look like you've seen a fight or two. Deal with the escaped creature by the mountain outflow spring.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on, what's in it for me?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: What do you want?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Access to the city sounds nice.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Fine, get to it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On secound thoughts, I don't like your attitude.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Then go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should head back to Attala and let her know that the aqueduct is secure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good work team! Let's let Attala know we all killed it, together!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Technically I helped kill it... Without me, the others might have been overwhelmed... Yes... I should let Attala know the aqueduct is secure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've taken care of the... What was that thing anyway?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: A spirit of sorts. Something far older than anything your people have bothered to remember. In any case, a deal is a deal. You may enter the city of Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: What about me?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Are you still here?!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Yes, and I'm not going anywhere!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Fine! If it'll make you stop pestering me, you can join...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You can join [player name].

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: [player name], he's your responsibility now. If either of you cause any trouble, you're both out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, hang on...

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. I'm Zuma, by the way. I'll meet you inside.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Thanks for that, mate. You should speak to Jess inside. She'll be in the north of the city. She could use a problem solver like yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Jess?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: She's the archaeologist in charge of the dig into these ruins they've found. Discovering the mysteries of the past and that.

sections.title: Talking to the Builders after Zuma departs

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: You should head inside, mate. Remember, Jess will be in the north of the city. She could use a problem solver like yourself.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Thanks for that, mate. You should speak to Jess inside. She'll be in the north of the city. She could use a problem solver like yourself.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: To me!

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: To you!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Keep it down!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm watching you, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to help, you know...

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I do?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Every day they let more of your kind in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no need for that.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Another human? Do what you need to and then be gone from here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was unnecessary.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the ruins before talking to Jessamine

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Excuse me! I don't believe I've given you permission to enter the dig.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Well with the suitation outside dealt with, I now need to keep an eye on this expedition. Any mistakes made in these ruins could bring the ground out from beneath our city!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it really that dangerous in there?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: It might be if you don't know what you're dealing with. Your people often assume they do, which generally makes things worse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The rest of your people don't seem very happy I'm here.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The rest?! I'm not happy you're here either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped you with the aqueduct. You could afford to show a little bit of gratitude.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You'll have my gratitude when you finish you business in Cam Torum and leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will the city's defense be okay without you?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: As I say, the situation has been dealt with for now. Besides, the only reason that situation existed in the first place is because of these ruins.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: At this point a smart person would call off this dig entirely, but as that doesn't seem set to happen, I'd say it's in our best interests we keep an eye on things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it you don't get on well with the humans?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: It's not exactly that. You have your virtues, but you have short sight and even shorter memories, which can be very dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not share what you know with us then?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I suspect some of your people wouldn't be too pleased if we did. The truth isn't something that everyone desires.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm always open to a bit of truth myself.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph! I doubt that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Thank you for your help back there, [player name]. Dwarves can be very stubborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can you, apparently.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It is not stubbornness on my part. It is duty that drives me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Duty to what?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I am a nelta of Ranul and I am bound by his tenets. One of them is to ensure that knowledge is protected and respected. These ruins they've found could contain all manner of things! I must find out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you hear about these ruins?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: The cave-in that revealed them shook the foundations of the Teomat!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: The dwarves tried to hide them from us, but we eventually found out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you expecting to find down there?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I don't know exactly yet, but knowledge of some kind! Maybe even some undiscovered history regarding Ralos and Ranul!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it you don't get on well with the dwarves?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: They are arrogant! They think they know it all, and worst of all, they are non-believers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Technically I'm a non-believer.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: But that is because you are ignorant of the truth, not because you outright reject it. Would you like me to tell you of the ways of Ranul?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Timoiva.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Nilsal! The name's Jessamine, though most people call me Jess. You must be [player name]. I hear you sorted out the situation holding back the arrivial of our supplies. Thanks for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't mention it. So you're here for these ruins beneath the city?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Yes, a cave-in revealed them! I'm here to lead the dig and see what we can discover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There seems to be a lot of ancient ruins popping up lately.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Gielinor has been around for a long time. We're really only just starting to scratch its surface!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I be of any help with the dig?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Well it seems our expedition has already gained a couple of new recruits somehow, but what I really need is someone more proactive who can fend for themselves. You seem perfect for the job!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Meet me inside the ruins. We need help setting up some base camps for further exploration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come the dwarves let you in?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: I work at the museum in Fortis. The dwarves have a begrudging alliance with the capital, and so they begrudgingly let me in.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: They demanded agreement to some absurdly expensive export deals in exchange, and even exclusive rights to some imports from the mainland.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: It took weeks of negotiation, and they still don't seem entirely satisfied.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it you don't get on too well?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: No. It's a shame. They would be a great source of historical knowledge, if they were just more willing to share it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: See you later.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine again before entering the ruins

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Nilsal! When you're ready, meet me inside the ruins. We need help setting up some base camps for further exploration.

sections.title: Attempting to enter any entrance before speaking to anyone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably speak to the others before I go exploring.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: G'day, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm surprised to see you both in such high spirits after walking past all those jeering dwarves.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Ah, well it's unfortunate, but no reason to be sad. It's them that ought to be sad.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Aye, mate. Livin' a life unable to connect with others because they look different must be miserable.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: We wish 'em the best. Hopefully their paths to personal growth are near and smooth.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine before talking to Attala and Zuma

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Xali.... Ates... Hmm, no, not quite right...

sections.title: Talking to Zuma before talking to Attala

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: The moon blotting out the sun, could this be...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Concerning...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you found?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: This is an old place, [player name]. Older even than much of what we dwarves remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's exciting.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: No, it's dangerous is what it is.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Now where is that fool priest?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Incredible...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you found?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: This is sacred ground. This is most obviously a shrine built by emissaries of Ranul.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You see a picture of a creature eating the sun and instead of a warning, you see evidence of your god? You really are beyond saving.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Enough! This mural may depict a scene straight from our holy scriptures! Show some respect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, calm down, both of you. Let's go see what the actual archaeologist has to say.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Have you finished setting up the camps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Let me know when you have. In the meantime, I've got to keep working on this inscription, whilst also making sure these two don't kill each other...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're... er... Very passionate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of which, what about you? Why a life of archaeology?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: I've always loved it. When I was a child, I had a friend in Fortis who I'd collect old trinkets with.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It's right here! It clearly says...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess it was a natural next step then?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I don't care what your book says! Your book won't keep us safe from...

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Indeed. The employees at the museum already knew me well by the time I applied. I've spent a lot of my life browsing it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The last time you humans did something like this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should visit the museum in Varrock some time. There's a lot to see!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Always 'you humans'. You do realise I am not my great-grandfather...

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: I've been meaning to! Thing is, I couldn't turn down this opportunity when it came up.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Keep this up and I'll have you kicked out of the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Understandable. Right, I should get back to those camps.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: The secrets of this dungeon belong to the Varlamorian people! You can't...

sections.title: Talking to either Attala or Zuma again

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: How can you make those claims so confidently?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It's in the holy scriptures! As I was saying...

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Can't get a word in edgeways with these two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're... er... Very passionate.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Strange...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you found?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: This mural has an inscription beneath it, written in the language of the Old Ones. I think I'm getting there, but I'm going to need more time to decipher it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: We don't need to be deciphering inscriptions to know that we shouldn't be here. I mean look at that... whatever that floating thing is. This place is dangerous!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: All the more reason to find out more about it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you need me to do anything in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I help with the translations? I actually have a bit of experience with this language.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: That's very kind of you to offer, but if it's okay with you, I actually have a different task I could use your help with.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: The surrounding caverns are quite large. If you could take some building supplies and set up some camps in each of them, it would make further exploration a lot easier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

sections.title: Attempting to enter an entrance without the building supplies

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should grab the camp building supplies before I do any more exploration.

sections.title: Attempting to enter a boss entrance

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This entrance looks dangerous. I probably shouldn't go in here without a good reason.

sections.title: Trapping rocks, collecting from grubby saplings, fishing, or rustling bushes before building the camps

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably shouldn't touch anything until the others have had a chance to look around.

sections.title: Talking to the builders after setting up all three camps

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: G'day, mate.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Thanks for taking care of those base camps, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. I hope there's no hard feelings over Jessamine asking me to do a part of your jobs.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: That's no worries, mate, I've no issue admitting you're better cut out for building camps in dangerous dungeons than I am.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: We may not be mul'i-disciplined adventurers like you, but we don't need to be. The world needs more than just heroes, don't it?

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Aye, mate, I'm content and proud to just be a hard working, decent builder.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Besides, without us, building supplies wouldn't be there in the first place. Life's all about working together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad to hear it. Keep up the good work then!

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: You too, mate.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine after setting up all three camps
Talking to Jessamine before getting the two talismans

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: You'll need to make some space for those two talismans [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Attala before getting the two talismans

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You'll need to make some space for those two talismans [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Zuma before getting the two talismans

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: You'll need to make some space for those two talismans [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished setting up the camps.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Thanks. I'm all done deciphering the inscription. Some of it has worn away, so we're missing bits, but between it, the murals and the big floating monolith, we've already found some pretty exciting stuff!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does the inscription say?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: 'Here in Neypotzli, by ??? of earth and streams, have these emissaries of ??? been sealed, ??? ??? ???.'

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Emissaries of Ranul! We must work to free them at once.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: First they built the place, now they're sealed in it. These leaping assumptions are going to get us all killed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I've only ever had good experiences opening ancient vaults.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Attala is right to be cautious. These murals don't look all too friendly...

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: But these emissaries may have ancient knowledge lost to the ages! We must investigate further.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: These ruins are directly beneath the Teomat... I suppose it's possible there may be some links to Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I will not allow you to put Cam Torum in danger just because you're curious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if these disagreements are really getting us anywhere...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You make a good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: I think it would be unwise to go and start freeing emissaries that we know nothing about.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Agreed!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: But we should still try and learn more about them. They may pose a risk to Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph! Very well.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: We know they were sealed by the power of earth and streams. This magic presumably originates from somewhere within these ruins. I also wonder if these talismans I found might be connected...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmm... Nahta will know what to do with these. [player name], take them to him in Cam Torum's magic shop. Tell him I sent you.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Come back to me when you have space and I'll give you the talismans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does the western mural depict?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: It's raining blood! It is clearly a depiction of death!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: It's not clear from the mural whether the creature depicted is calling the rain or lamenting its arrival.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It's an emissary of the blood moon! A bringer of rain and bountiful harvests!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Blood moons don't usually bring good harvests, and I don't see any thriving crops depicted in the mural.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you make of it?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: You cannot deny us the Blood Moon's bounty!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: The blood moon is nothing more than a cosmic occurrence. We need more context to come to any conclusions.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Cam Torum has no interest in bathing in blood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does the eastern mural depict?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: An emissary of the eclipsed moon! A herald of Ximoua!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ximoua? It is eating the sun, you fool! If it's a herald of anything, it's death!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Given what is taught about Ximoua, that isn't too surprising.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is Ximoua again?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: An eclipse that marks the end of a cycle! The destruction of the world and its rebirth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I remember now. Very pleasant.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Indeed, it is an event we all look forward to. It is said that when it happens, Ralos and Ranul unite all life as one! Can you imagine such an honour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe we should focus on something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does the northern mural depict?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: A creature snapping a mountain in half. Care to explain this one, Zuma?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: It's an emissary of the blue moon! Clearly... er... bringing an end to the winter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on this one, Jess?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Blue moons are exceedinly rare... They're only seen occasionally during volcanic eruptions. It's possible that this cracking mountain signals the appearance of a volcano.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine before finishing the talismans

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Do you have the enchanted talismans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still working on it.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Let Attala know if you need any help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good job mediating those two.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Thanks. It does feel a bit like I'm walking a tightrope over a scorpion nest at times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Attala might be worse than a pit of scorpions!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Did I hear my name?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Yes! We were just saying how helpful you've been!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Quite!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you end up getting assigned to these ruins?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: The museum suspected there might be an Old One connection. It's one of my specialties, so exploring these kinds of places is what I do.

sections.title: Talking to Attala before finishing the talismans

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Take the talismans to Nahta in Cam Torum's magic shop. Tell him I sent you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I lost them?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Then you'll need to find some more yourself, and take better care with them once you have!

sections.title: Talking to Zuma before finishing the talismans

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Any word on the enchanted talismans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still working on it.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I'm afraid you'll need to talk to Attala if you need any help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What lead you to be such a devout follower of Ranul?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Ralos appeals to many who wish to live fast-paced lives.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I do see that appeal, but there is a calmness and peace in Ranul that calls to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't Ranul govern over death? That doesn't put you off?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Making peace with death is one of the things that attracted me to Ranul's teachings.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: That, and all the time I get to spend in nice and quiet libraries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is this place what you were expecting?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Far more so! As much as Attala refuses to believe it, I think these ruins are connected to Ranul.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it because of all the moon symbology?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: That's part of it, but there's also that thing at the centre of this room. Some of the markings remind me of something I once read about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, if a bit vague. Care to elaborate on what it is you read about?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I can't really. I'm still not done putting my theory together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I look forward to hearing more once you've done so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Timoiva.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Are you in need of some runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've been sent by Attala.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Attala? Why's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're trying to find out more about a source of magic in the ruins beneath the city. She asked me to bring some talismans to you.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: If she needs me to get involved, then no doubt this is something well beyond your people and probably highly dangerous.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: But I suppose if she's okay with that... Go on then, let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I seem to have misplaced them.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Then you'd better go and find them.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: How in the... Where did you get these?

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Actually, on second thoughts, I'm not sure I want to know.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: There. Now take them to the blacksmith. Tell him they've been enchanted to react to naguanil magic and they need some finishing touches.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Naguanil? What's that?

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: A type of magic your people have forgotten, alongside many other things. I don't have time to catch you up on the history of the word though, so you'll need to learn more somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the help.

sections.title: Using a water or earth talisman on Nahta

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Are you in need of some runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've been sent by Attala.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: And I already attuned your talismans. Take them to the blacksmith. Tell him they've been enchanted to react to naguanil magic and they need some finishing touches.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Are you in need of some runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've lost one of the talismans you gave me.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Well luckily for you, I expected this might happen.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Make some room and I'll get you sorted out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've lost the talismans you gave me.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Well luckily for you, I expected this might happen.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Make some room and I'll get you sorted out.

lines.speaker: Nahta
lines.text: Are you in need of some runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've been sent by Attala.

sections.title: Talking to Attala after getting the enchanted talismans

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You're meant to be in Cam Torum sorting those talismans out. Return here once you have done so.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: A human? Either buy something or get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with these talismans.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: I'm sure you could use my help with many things, human, but I'm very busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was sent by Attala.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Attala? What's she got to do with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're investigating whether the ruins beneath the city pose any threat to its citizens.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Oh... Well I suppose I can help then, as long as you make it quick. What did you say you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The owner of the magic shop said something about these talismans detecting... aguatil?

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Naguanil. Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help?

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Of course. This sort of magic is child's play. Where are the talismans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I seem to have lost them.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Then stop wasting my time!

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Of course. This sort of magic is child's play. Give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: All done. Take these back to Attala. She'll tell you what to do with them.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Now, get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about magic? Aren't you a blacksmith?

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: I am one of the Dwarves of Cam Torum, human! Our metalwork and our magic are one. I am an expert in both.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds interesting. Could you teach me how to mix smithing and magic?

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Hah, that's a good joke!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... But can you help with the talismans?

sections.title: Using an enchanted talisman on the blacksmith

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: A human? Either buy something or get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with these talismans.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: I already told you to take them back to Attala. She'll tell you what to do with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: A human? Either buy something or get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've lost one of the talismans you gave me.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Humans! You're lucky I prepared for this!

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Make some room if you want my help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the talismans you gave me.

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Humans! You're lucky I prepared for this!

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: Make some room if you want my help!

lines.speaker: Blacksmith
lines.text: A human? Either buy something or get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with these talismans.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine after enchanting the talismans

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Do you have the enchanted talismans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I managed to get the dwarves to cooperate for just long enough to make them.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: That's no small feat! You should speak to Attala about it.

sections.title: Talking to Zuma after enchanting the talismans

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Any word on the enchanted talismans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. The dwarves of Cam Torum are quite skilled, so it was relatively quick.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Hmph! All the skill in the world couldn't make up for their attitude.

sections.title: Talking to Attala after infusing the talismans

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Have you managed to find the source of these earth and stream magics yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Remember, the talismans should guide you to the point in this place where these magics resonate most strongly.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Of course, we've not tried something like this before, so I imagine it will require you to do some testing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the enchanted talismans.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Good. Now let's not waste any more time. In theory, the talismans should guide you to the point in this place where these magics of earth and streams resonate most strongly.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Of course, we've not tried something like this before, so I imagine it will require you to do some testing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to be well known by the other dwarves in Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Of course. I am a key advisor to Highlord Katlo himself. Few dwarves hold as much sway as I do. Not just anyone can authorise the entry of a human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I should thank you for letting me in then.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph! Don't make me regret it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your friends weren't very happy to see me.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: That doesn't surprise me. They're busy people with much less patience for humans than I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have patience for humans?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine after talking to Attala about the infused talismans

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Jess.

sections.title: Talking to Zuma after talking to Attala about the infused talismans

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: How goes the search, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad, thank you.

sections.title: Locating the talismans at the correct position

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Euat xita kemo? Euat iknil?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... What?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: A human? Interesting... In that case, allow me to use a language more familiar to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I think. So who are you?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I am Eyatlalli, a nagua of earth and streams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... And what is this place?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Neypotzli. A prison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A prison? For who? You?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: For the Moons of Peril, and their long dead summoner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Moons of Peril?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes. Powerful and terrible nagua summoned to wreak havoc.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would anyone want to do that?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: In the face of inevitable death, a powerful shaman decided to lash out and inflict death on others.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Her fellows disagreed with her actions. They created this prison, and they summoned me to watch over it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The icosahedron...

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine after meeting Eyatlalli
Talking to Jessamine, Attala, or Eyatlalli after Eyatlalli appears

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: [player name], what's going on?!

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The Moons of Peril rage against their bindings. Holding them back is exhausting. Please bring me the bream scales, moss lizard tail and moonlight grub paste from elsewhere in Neypotzli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find bream?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: They can be fished in the north east section of Neypotzli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find moss lizards?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: They can be caught in the north west section of Neypotzli. They'll likely be hiding in the bushes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find moonlight grubs?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: They can be picked off of the saplings growing all around the north of Neypotzli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: [player name], what's going on?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hang on... is that...

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I am Eyatlalli, a nagua of-

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Never mind the introductions, what was that shaking?!

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The icosahedron has been disturbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The icosa-who?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Icosahedron. An object of twenty facets, floating at the centre of this room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did anybody touch that thing whilst I was gone?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Zuma did something. We were discussing the murals and when we turned around, he was gone!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I knew I shouldn't have let that fool priest in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does the icosahedron do?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It is key to the magics that keep the Moons of Peril contained. It reshapes and reweaves the magical seals as the Moons attempt to break them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't it a good thing it moved then?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: For it to have moved means that the seals must have needed reshaping. That has not happened for decades.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Zuma must have done something!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we fix it?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: For centuries, the magic used to seal the Moons has been woven into Neypotzli and the creatures that inhabit it.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Naguanil is the magic of nature. A ritual involving the living parts of this place could re-infuse the icosahedron.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Living parts? As in the animals?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes. Bring me scales of a bream, the tail of a moss lizard and paste made of moonlight grubs. I will begin preparing the rest of the ritual.

sections.title: Talking to Eyatlalli with the items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the items.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Good. Arrange them as follows:

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Place the scales in the shape of the sun at high noon.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Place the lizard tail beneath it as the earth.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Now carve the earth in two with the paste, as the stream carves the earth.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The ritual is in motion, but the wound to the seal will not close with the Moons still raging against it. I must ask you for one more small favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, no...

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The Moons cannot be killed, but they can be distracted whilst I repair the seal. Enter their chambers here in Neypotzli and engage them in combat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't these things like unstoppable forces of nature or something? Am I going to be alright in there?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Ordinarily, no. Their mere presence is enough to bring most creatures to an early death. However, you have my help.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Whilst you fight them, look for my glyphs. They will protect and guide you. If the Moons become too enraged, I will transport you out of their chambers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remind me what your glyphs look like?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Here. This is what to look for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I expect from the Blood Moon?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: When it starts to rain blood, look to your feet. When the jaguars are summoned, prepare to leap back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I expect from the Blue Moon?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Be prepared to fight without your weapon. Ensure the room's braziers remain lit, for the cold will restore the Moon's strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I expect from the Eclipse Moon?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Ensure it doesn't catch you with your back turned. Make sure to take cover when it fires its searing hot rays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Any time the Moons of Peril inflict pain, they can heal themselves, freeze your joints, or blind your eyes. A defensive approach should be used against them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

sections.title: Talking to Eyatlalli after placing the scales

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The Moons of Peril rage against their bindings. Holding them back is exhausting. Please bring me the moss lizard tail and moonlight grub paste from elsewhere in Neypotzli

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Place the lizard tail beneath it as the earth.

sections.title: Talking to Eyatlalli after placing the tail

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The Moons of Peril rage against their bindings. Holding them back is exhausting. Please bring me the moonlight grub paste from elsewhere in Neypotzli.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Now carve the earth in two with the paste, as the stream carves the earth.

sections.title: Talking to Eyatlalli after placing the grub paste

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The ritual is in motion, but the wound to the seal will not close with the Moons still raging against it. I must ask you for one more small favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is quite the dilemma.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: It is, although I'd be lying if I didn't admit that it is also fascinating.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Naguanil magic, ancient spirits of nature... This is the discovery of the century!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's make sure that Cam Torum is safe before we start celebrating.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What for?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: After berating you about keeping an eye on the priest, I failed to do the same myself. I was caught up in the mystery.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Perhaps there's a little human in me after all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, we'll figure this out.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: G'day, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem remarkably calm considering everything that's going on.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Life's unpredictable, mate. No sense stressin' over what's out of your control.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: It's not like we're not aware of the danger we're in, but worryin' about it ain't productive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shouldn't you be getting out of here if you feel like you're in danger?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: We may not be in total control of our surroundings, but we can still impact them positively through our actions. Like bein' here to support you.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Even if that's just emotionally. We believe in you, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: No worries, mate. Go get 'em.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. The Moons are sufficiently distracted. You should now return to me.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. The Blue Moon of Peril is sufficiently distracted. You should now proceed on to the next one.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. The Moons are sufficiently distracted. You should now return to me.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. The Blood Moon of Peril is sufficiently distracted. You should now proceed on to the next one.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I can't believe it... An emissary of my god, standing before me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zuma, no! Step back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get out of here!

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. The Moons are sufficiently distracted. You should now return to me.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Thank you, iknil. Eclipse Moon of Peril is sufficiently distracted. You should now proceed on to the next one.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the boss chambers

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've done everything I need to here. I should check in with Eyatlalli.

sections.title: Talking to Zuma or the builders

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I am a fool...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes, a bit.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: You meant well. You thought you were helpin'. Can't fault you for that part, mate.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: You made a mistake. Coulda been any of us. Just gotta make sure we learn from our mistakes is all.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: And if yer genuinely sorry, best you can do is work to be better, not mope around. So keep your chin up, mate!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Thank you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you do, anyway?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I saw sun and moon runes on that thing. Recalled something from Ranul's teachings, a rite he once performed...

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I tried to emulate it, and it worked. I thought I had freed his emissaries. But they weren't that at all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. Maybe there was some sort of connection between Ranul and this place after all.

sections.title: Talking to Jessamine after defeating the moons

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Did you do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Good work, [player name]. We made many significant discoveries today, even if it was a bit precarious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perilous, even.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: I'll be sticking around here for some time I reckon. There's still so much to discover in this place. See you around.

sections.title: Talking to Attala after defeating the moons

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The nagua seems to have relaxed. I assume you were successful?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You have my gratitude, [player name]. You have helped to ensure Cam Torum's safety. You will always be welcome back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something tells me the rest of your people won't be too happy about that.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Well it's not their choice, so they'll just have to suck it up. Who knows, maybe given long enough, you might start to grow on them!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I suppose I should think about heading back to the Teomat. I might not mention everything that happened down here to the others...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that's understandable.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: I suppose I still have plenty to share. Maybe I'll write a book about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure it will be a great one. Until next time, Zuma.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Well, all's well that ends well.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: We never had any doubts! Always knew you'd get it sorted, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: No worries, mate.

sections.title: Talking to Eyatlalli after defeating the moons

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: You have done well. The seal is repaired, but the Moons have seen a glimpse of freedom. Even now they rage against their bindings. It is... taxing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: You could. Distracting them again will afford me some rest. In return, I am willing to repay you with trinkets from ancient times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds interesting.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Very well. Come and claim your rewards from the chamber to the south east. Should you aid in containing them again, you can return there to receive more rewards.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: For now though, thank you for your help, iknil.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you ever get tired of guarding this place?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Care to explain further?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Does the stream tire of eroding the earth? Does the earth tire of forming the mountain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not that I can tell.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I have more in common with them than I do with most of your kind, iknil. Think of me as you do them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This place is beautiful. I thought it was supposed to be a prison?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It is a prison. Everything around us serves that purpose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even the plants and the streams?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I am a nagua of earth and stream. Being close to both empowers me to do my task.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Just as being far from moonlight weakens the Moons of Peril.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: Good to see you again, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you both as well. How's things?

lines.speaker: Builder 2
lines.text: Never better, mate. Never better. This here ruin was the find of the century!

lines.speaker: Builder 1
lines.text: All credit to Jess, o'course, but we're glad to be doing our bit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty good, thanks. How about you? Made any more discoveries down here?

lines.speaker: Jessamine
lines.text: More than you can count! I've found so much new information on the Old Ones and naguanil. The museum are hoping to include it all in some new exhibits soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds awesome! I'll be sure to keep an eye out for them. On that note, I'll let you get back to it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Now that the situation in the ruins is under control, things are nice and quiet, which is just what we need. That whole buisness down there got a lot of dwarves riled up.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Naturally, most blame you humans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. Have you tried explaining to them that we actually helped?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ha! Good one. You think I'm going to make a laughing stock of myself by daring to claim a human did something positive? Keep dreaming!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how long has Cam Torum been around?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: What am I, a historian?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but you're the only dwarf here that might be willing to share that sort of information with me.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph. Very well. The Varlamorians won't mention this, but we were the first to settle here. Our ancestors saw the writing on the wall when Xeric came to power in Kourend. They chose not to stick around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Must have been a big thing for them, leaving their home.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Doubtful. My people are all too familiar with that sort of thing. The Lovakengj dwarves have mostly forgotten their history, but we Dwarves of Cam Torum remember it all too well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remember what?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Before Lovakengj, there was Imcandoria. Our ancestors there were forced to abandon their home, just as we were forced to leave Kourend.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: If you humans had had your way, we might have had to abandon Cam Torum by now as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't know that.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: But I do know that. As I said, we remember our history, and we know that those who don't are doomed to repeat it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The Varlamorians nearly destroyed themselves in their War of Betrayal, but it was not them who were betrayed, it was us.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I was a young dwarf when their war risked destroying our home. I saw the results of their pride. Meanwhile, they've all but forgotten it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. I didn't mean to dig up old wounds.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph. It's fine. Your intentions are good, [player name], which is more than can be said for most of your kind. Who knows, maybe one or two dwarves here might even grow to tolerate you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zuma. How are things?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: A lot less stressful up here. I think I might leave the adventuring to people like you from now on.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Still, at least it gave me a lot to talk about. Those Moons of Peril are concerning, but exciting! I just wish we knew more about their potential links to Ranul.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you still think there is a link?

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Oh yes. It may not have been the one I originally thought, but there must be something! They're called Moons after all!

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: The all-powerful Ranul wouldn't have just let some random creatures take on a name like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... It's an interesting thought.

lines.speaker: Zuma
lines.text: Indeed. And on that note, I have some research to do! Until next time.
